summary: Make `workflow archive` sync the CLI's known resources instead of reporting a zero-resource success.
reason: `workflow archive` was hardcoded to an empty resource slice, so it always printed a successful archive summary with 0 items across 0 resources. The command now uses the same known sync resource list accepted by `sync --resources` and keeps an explicit no-resource warning for specs that genuinely have no sync targets.
validated_outcome: `go test ./...`, `cli-printing-press publish validate`, `verify-skill`, `verify-publish-package`, and `verify-supply-chain` pass with regression coverage proving `workflow archive --json` requests every known sync resource.
